short_description: Business loans from ₹5 Lakh to ₹10 Lakh for established micro-enterprises without collateral.
detailed_description: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) provides loans to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises under three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishor (₹50,001 to ₹5 Lakh), and Tarun (₹5 Lakh to ₹10 Lakh). The Tarun category is designed for well-established micro-enterprises that need growth capital. These loans are provided by banks, NBFCs, and MFIs without requiring collateral, making it easier for entrepreneurs to expand their businesses.
key_benefits: Flexible repayment tenure up to 7 years
Available for manufacturing, trading, and service sectors
Working capital and term loan both available
other_conditions: Business should be in manufacturing, trading, or service sector
Applicant should have existing business operations
Business should be a non-corporate, non-farm enterprise
Business plan and financial projections required
Should not be defaulter in any bank

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Detailed project report with financial projections
GST registration, Shop Act license, or business registration certificate
For machinery or equipment purchase (if applicable)

application_steps_online: Visit MUDRA portal or your bank's website
Fill the form with business and personal details
Prepare detailed business plan and financial projections
Submit application online or visit bank branch
Bank will assess and process the application
Upon approval, loan amount will be disbursed
application_steps_offline: Visit nearest bank, NBFC, or MFI offering MUDRA loans
Collect MUDRA Tarun loan application form
Fill the form with accurate business details
Attach all required documents and business plan
Submit to loan officer for assessment
Attend interview if required by bank
Receive loan disbursement in bank account

submission_locations.address: Any commercial bank, RRB, cooperative bank, NBFC, or MFI
submission_locations.notes: Different banks may have different interest rates and processing fees

renewal_required: No (but can apply for fresh loan after repayment)

faq.question: What is the interest rate for Tarun loans?
faq.answer: Interest rates vary by lender, typically ranging from 10% to 12% per annum. Check with your bank for current rates.

faq.question: Is collateral required for Tarun loans?
faq.answer: No, MUDRA loans including Tarun category do not require collateral or third-party guarantee.

faq.question: Can I get both working capital and term loan?
faq.answer: Yes, MUDRA Tarun can be used for both working capital needs and term loan for assets, within the ₹10 Lakh limit.

common_rejection_reasons: Poor credit history or existing defaults
tips_for_successful_application: Prepare comprehensive business plan with realistic projections
Show existing business operations and revenue
Apply through bank where you have existing relationship
Be prepared to explain how loan will be utilized
